fundamentals.name: DoorDash, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: DoorDash, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, operates a commerce platform that connects merchants, consumers, and dashers in the United States and internationally. The company operates DoorDash Marketplace, Wolt Marketplace, and Deliveroo Marketplace, which provide various services, such as customer acquisition, demand generation, order fulfillment, merchandising, payment processing, and customer support. It also offers consumer membership programs, DashPass, Wolt+, and Deliveroo Plus; advertising as a value-added service through its marketplaces; and white-label delivery fulfillment services, as well as services that help merchants establish online ordering, build branded mobile apps, manage reservations and in-store dining, manage consumer relationships, enable tableside order and pay, and improve customer support. The company was formerly known as Palo Alto Delivery Inc. and changed its name to DoorDash, Inc. in 2015. DoorDash, Inc. was founded in 2013 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

judgment.summary: DoorDash shows strong revenue growth and healthy cash flow but faces challenges with declining earnings growth and a high valuation multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of $0.42 vs $0.36 expected; next earnings date is 2026-05-06.
positives: Revenue growth 33.1% YoY indicates strong market adoption.
Free cash flow of $2.30B TTM demonstrates robust operational cash generation.
Gross margin at 51.8% and operating margin at 5.3% show good unit economics.
Forward P/E of 20.24x is significantly lower than trailing P/E of 74.68x, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target of $245.99.
negatives: Earnings growth is negative at -6.1% YoY, and quarterly earnings growth is -4.7%.
The PEG ratio of 3.15 suggests the stock may be overvalued relative to its earnings growth.
Return on equity at 9.9% is modest.
Beta of 1.87 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: DoorDash (DASH) is seeing mixed signals following a week where Jim Cramer highlighted it as an investment opportunity, while Uber made a $11.6 billion bid for Delivery Hero, a rival. Despite the positive commentary from Cramer and a "now undervalued" assessment from GuruFocus, the stock experienced a 3.9% decline. The Uber bid, while potentially impacting the competitive landscape, is framed by analysts as a strategic move for future-proofing against automation rather than an immediate threat to DoorDash.

headlines.title: Uber Stock Falls After $11.6 Billion Bid For Delivery Hero. Here's Why Analysts Say Deal Makes Sense.
